verdicts.mechanism: Line 86's inverted condition skips charset_normalizer validation when present (falling through to the wrong warning) and instead calls `charset_normalizer_version.split(".")` when it's None, raising an uncaught AttributeError (not caught by the AssertionError/ValueError except at line 118) when neither chardet nor charset_normalizer is installed.
Line 942-943 catches DecodeError and passes silently instead of raising ContentDecodingError, unlike ProtocolError→ChunkedEncodingError (940-941) and other exceptions that are properly converted.

Line 1017 uses `(pending) - (chunk)` where pending/chunk are str or bytes; the `-` operator is unsupported for these types, so a TypeError is raised on the second iteration whenever a prior chunk left a pending fragment (i.e., whenever `pending is not None` at line 1015), instead of concatenating with `+`.

`if not ("proxies" not in kwargs):` reduces to `if "proxies" in kwargs:`, the opposite of the intended `if "proxies" not in kwargs:` setdefault-style guard, so explicit per-call proxies are overwritten by resolve_proxies() while calls without proxies never get self.proxies/env-resolved proxies set at all before adapter.send().

Line 787 computes `(preferred_clock()) + (start)` instead of subtracting start from the end time, so `r.elapsed` is set to a bogus sum (~2x current epoch time in seconds) rather than the actual request duration.

Line 122 applies the `-` operator between two `str` objects; `str.__sub__` doesn't exist, so this raises `TypeError` unconditionally at import time since `_init()` is called at line 128 and `__doc__` is not None (docstring defined at lines 1-19).

The except block at lines 336-338 catches BaseException, removes the temp file, but ends with bare `pass` instead of `raise`, so per Python semantics the exception is not propagated and the `with atomic_open(...)` block silently succeeds despite the underlying I/O error.

range(1, len(parts)-1) skips index len(parts)-1, the last split segment, so it's never re-prefixed with '%' in the else branch nor decoded in the if branch (parts[i] = c + ... or parts[i] = '%' + parts[i] never executes for it), so join() silently drops the '%' from the final percent-escape sequence, e.g. 'abc%20def'.split('%') -> ['abc','20def'] with range(1,1) empty yields 'abc20def'.
